helpful: The provisioned full petition and brief in opposition exposed both the doctrinal argument and vehicle defects.
The statpack supplied paid-petition, CVSG, relist, Term, and originating-court calibration.
CourtListener MCP confirmed how a post-Cedar Point appellate decision treated PruneYard.
gaps: SCOTUS corpus rows lack subject-topic metadata, so the corpus query returned broad recent grants rather than close property-rights cert comparators.
A compact historical feature-matched cert lookup covering amici, interlocutory posture, and requests to overrule precedent would improve calibration.
notes: The corpus lookup required 153 ranged GETs and about 40 MB for ten broad rows. The CourtListener MCP was available and no REST fallback was used.
